name: tenant-c
description: Edge-case coverage tenant. Every Graph CA policy shape we know about appears at least once: country named-location includes, IP named-location excludes, app filters, device platform conditions, sign-in frequency controls, persistent browser session controls, AND/OR grant combinations, workload identity policies, and role-scoped policies for Global Admin and Privileged Role Admin.
notes: calibrated against rules engine v0.0.1; every Graph shape parses without rule crashes.

signin_scenarios.name: baseline
signin_scenarios.description: 30 days of normal behaviour, no injected anomalies.

signin_scenarios.name: replay-attacks
signin_scenarios.description: Baseline plus scripted impossible-travel, MFA bypass, off-hours admin, credential stuffing, new ASN.

signin_scenarios.name: noisy
signin_scenarios.description: Operationally noisy normals plus the same scripted attacks.

anomaly_calibration.notes: Sprint 3.5b restore. Pooled topology is the default; per-user topology and off-hours-admin rule are gated. The Sprint 3.5 noisy-recall slip (0.741) is gone (now 0.889) because the pooled IF generalises better against the noise injection than per-user models trained on each user's smaller distribution. Numbers track Sprint 3 metadata; small deltas reflect library-version drift.
